mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, directly lifting S&P 500 valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid drop in the unemployment rate below 9.5% within three months signals a labor market inflection that boosts consumer spending expectations, driving positive equity returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE drops below 9.5%, or the signal shows no predictive power over a 5-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in housing starts and retail sales indicates broad-based consumer demand expansion, which increases corporate earnings expectations and pushes the S&P 500 higher over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces a negative direction-adjusted return spread greater than 1.5% in backtests, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: An industrial production index exceeding 90% of its historical peak signals supply-side capacity utilization reaching critical levels, which typically precedes inflationary pressures and subsequent equity valuation compression over a quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns remain positive in over 70% of observations where INDPRO exceeds 90% of its peak, or the signal's information ratio falls below 0.2 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth consistently above 2.5% annualized rate reflects robust aggregate demand, which supports corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: The hypothesis fails if 5-day forward returns show no statistical difference between high-GDP and low-GDP regimes, or if the rolling correlation between GDP growth and SPX returns drops below 0.1 for two consecutive years.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate crossing above 0.25% marks the onset of monetary tightening, which increases borrowing costs and compresses equity multiples, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the signal exhibits a negative Sharpe ratio over a 10-year walk-forward evaluation.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous payroll growth exceeding 150,000 and industrial production rising above 85% indicates synchronized labor and supply recovery, which historically drives strong equity momentum over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields a negative alpha greater than 100 basis points per quarter, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for FEDFUNDS changes in a multivariate factor model.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in housing starts above 550,000 units signals durable consumer confidence and construction sector strength, which typically lifts financial sector earnings and pushes the S&P 500 higher over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero when HOUST exceeds 550,000, or the signal's predictive accuracy drops below 52% in a 5-year out-of-sample test.
